title: Shipped the write path, never wired the read path — the feature recorded data nobody ever read back
summary: A failure-memory feature stored records perfectly, but no query path ever loaded them: lookups and reports ran only against the built-in corpus. The product's core loop was silently open for a full release cycle while all write-side tests were green.
mistake: Verifying a data feature by testing only the write side (record succeeds, file exists, schema valid) and declaring it done. Nothing asserted that a consumer actually loads what was written, so the recall half was never wired: recorded data was invisible to every read endpoint, and the gap survived reviews because each half looked complete on its own.
instead: For every write path, name the read path in the same change and prove the loop with one end-to-end test: write through the public API, then assert the written record is visible through each consumer that should see it (query, report, recall). Audit question that catches this class in review: 'who reads this, and where is the test that reads it?' Also verify the loop in the PRODUCTION wiring (the binary/config actually deployed), not just in unit scope.
